title: MAGNESIUM L-THREONATE
description: Magnesium L-Threonate is a patented form of magnesium chelated with L-threonic acid (a metabolite of vitamin C) that demonstrates superior blood-brain barrier permeability compared to other magnesium forms. Research indicates it effectively elevates magnesium concentrations in brain tissue and cerebrospinal fluid, potentially enhancing cognitive function, memory, and synaptic plasticity by modulating NMDA receptors and supporting neuronal health. It's primarily marketed for cognitive enhancement, neuroprotection, and age-related memory decline.

research.title: A Magtein®, Magnesium L-Threonate, -Based Formula Improves Brain Cognitive Functions in Healthy Chinese Adults
research.summary: Double-blind, placebo-controlled study of 109 adults aged 18-65 found that a magnesium L-threonate-based formulation significantly improved cognitive functions as measured by standardized clinical memory tests commonly used in Chinese hospitals.

research.title: L-Threonic Acid Magnesium Salt Supplementation in ADHD: An Open-Label Pilot Study
research.summary: Open-label pilot study with 15 adults found 47% met response criteria with significant improvements in ADHD symptoms, cognitive flexibility, and IQ performance measures over 12 weeks of supplementation.

research.title: Magnesium-L-threonate alleviate colonic inflammation and memory impairment in chronic-plus-binge alcohol feeding mice
research.summary: Animal study demonstrated that MgT supplementation reduced inflammatory cytokine expression and alleviated memory impairments in alcohol-exposed mice, suggesting protective effects against alcohol-mediated cognitive decline.

research.title: Magnesium supplement enhances spatial-context pattern separation and prevents fear overgeneralization
research.summary: Rat study showed MgT treatment enhanced ability to discriminate between similar contexts and improved pattern separation without enhancing fear memory itself, suggesting cognitive benefits beyond basic memory consolidation.

research.title: Targeting the NMDA receptor subunit NR2B for treating or preventing age-related memory decline
research.summary: Review highlighting that magnesium L-threonate can boost brain NR2B receptor expression, potentially reversing age-related memory deficits through enhanced synaptic plasticity mechanisms.

research.title: Elevation of Brain Magnesium Potentiates Neural Stem Cell Proliferation in the Hippocampus of Young and Aged Mice
research.summary: Study found both short- and long-term MgT supplementation increased hippocampal neural stem cell proliferation in young mice and prevented age-associated decline in older mice, suggesting neuroprotective and neurogenic effects.

research.title: Magnesium-L-threonate improves sleep quality and daytime functioning in adults with self-reported sleep problems: A randomized controlled trial
research.summary: Randomized controlled trial of 80 adults found 1g/day of MgT for 21 days significantly improved sleep quality metrics and daytime functioning compared to placebo in participants with self-reported sleep problems.

research.title: Magnesium-L-threonate treats Alzheimer's disease by modulating the microbiota-gut-brain axis
research.summary: Research demonstrated MgT has protective effects on learning and memory in Alzheimer's models, potentially working through modulation of the gut microbiome and gut-brain axis inflammation pathways.

sentiment.summary: Most users report significant cognitive improvements including enhanced focus, reduced brain fog, and better memory, though some experience anxiety or no effects. The high cost is a common complaint, but many find the benefits worth the premium price compared to cheaper magnesium forms.

effects.label: Response Variability:
effects.description: Effectiveness appears highly individual - works dramatically for some users within days, while others notice minimal to no benefits even after weeks of use

effects.label: Rapid Onset:
effects.description: Unlike some supplements requiring weeks to notice effects, many users report noticeable cognitive improvements within 1-7 days of starting

effects.label: Withdrawal Effects:
effects.description: Some users report significant cognitive decline when stopping, with brain fog and reduced mental clarity returning within a week, suggesting genuine neurological impact

effects.label: Comparison to Other Forms:
effects.description: Users who tried multiple magnesium types often report L-threonate provides unique cognitive benefits not achieved with glycinate, citrate, or oxide forms

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: Most users take 144-200mg elemental magnesium from L-threonate (typically 2000mg total compound), often split between morning and evening doses

dosage.label: Timing Considerations:
dosage.description: Mixed preferences - some take in morning for cognitive boost, others before bed for sleep, with individual response determining optimal timing

dosage.label: Cost-Effective Strategies:
dosage.description: Users note taking full research doses (2000mg compound) requires 3-4 capsules daily, making it expensive; some use lower doses or combine with cheaper magnesium forms

dosage.label: Loading Period:
dosage.description: Several users suggest benefits may take 1-4 weeks to fully manifest, though many report effects within the first week of consistent use

sideEffects.label: Anxiety Paradox:
sideEffects.description: Significant minority report increased anxiety, restlessness, or worsening of existing anxiety symptoms, particularly those sensitive to glycine or glutamatergic effects

sideEffects.label: Digestive Tolerance:
sideEffects.description: Generally well-tolerated digestively compared to citrate or oxide forms, with minimal laxative effects reported even at higher doses

sideEffects.label: Insomnia Risk:
sideEffects.description: Some users find it too mentally stimulating if taken in evening, causing difficulty falling asleep or overly active nighttime cognition

sideEffects.label: Headaches & Physical Symptoms:
sideEffects.description: Occasional reports of headaches, dehydration, or neck/back pain, though less common than cognitive side effects

availability.label: Premium Pricing:
availability.description: Consistently noted as expensive compared to other magnesium forms, typically $30-45 for 30-day supply, with concerns about affordability for long-term use

availability.label: Brand Recommendations:
availability.description: Users recommend products using the original Magtein® patented ingredient with research backing

availability.label: Generic Concerns:
availability.description: Users express skepticism about cheaper alternatives, questioning whether non-branded versions contain authentic L-threonate or effective forms, with preference for established brands despite higher cost
